text: Databases are essential for storing and managing information. There are two main types: SQL and NoSQL. SQL databases, like MySQL, organize data into tables with rows and columns. They rely on structured queries using SQL language to find and manipulate data. NoSQL databases, like MongoDB, are more flexible, storing data in documents or key-value pairs, which makes them suitable for unstructured data. Data modeling involves planning how data is structured, such as deciding table relationships in SQL or document fields in NoSQL. Indexes are tools that speed up queries by creating a reference for fast data retrieval. Understanding these concepts helps you choose the right database for your needs and write efficient queries.
